---
title: "prexorctl cluster recover"
description: "Walks the operator through cluster recovery scenarios"
---

<!-- auto-generated by tools/gen-cli-docs.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

Walks the operator through cluster recovery scenarios.
Quorum-preserved (≤ floor((N-1)/2) failures): force-ejects dead peers from the
member list via the cluster API. Repeats prexorctl cluster eject under the hood.
Catastrophic (quorum lost): prints the offline single-survivor reset procedure.
This is destructive filesystem surgery on a stopped controller — see
docs/runbooks/recover-cluster.md for the canonical playbook.

## Usage

```text
prexorctl cluster recover [flags]
```

## Flags

```text
--eject strings          Comma-separated dead nodeIds to eject (skips the interactive prompt)
    -h, --help                   help for recover
        --i-have-only-survivor   Print the catastrophic single-survivor reset playbook (quorum is lost)
        --yes                    Skip the interactive confirmation
```

## Global flags

```text
--ascii               Use ASCII glyphs only (no unicode box drawing, sparklines, etc.)
        --context string      Override active context for this invocation
    -c, --controller string   Override controller URL
    -j, --json                Output in JSON format
        --no-color            Disable colored output
    -t, --token string        Override auth token
    -v, --verbose             Show HTTP request/response details
```
